title: Bueno Sopaipillas
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bffcg","text":"Sopaipillas are delicious, oily dough pockets. A New Mexican staple. Eat them as a dessert with honey, or as an entree, stuffing the sopaipilla with meat, beans, cheese, rice, etc. The options are near limitless! This protocol describes the steps to make sopaipillas using a box of mix from the BUENO foods company.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"8gcta","text":"Box of Sopaipilla Mix, Oil, Water, Flour, Mixing Bowl","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: YOU WILL NEED 1 Box of Bueno Sopaipilla Mix 1 Tbsp vegetable oil 3/4 cup warm water 3 cups vegetable oil (for frying)
EMPTY entire contents of sopaipilla mix into a large bowl.
ADD 1 Tbsp. oil. Gradually add water. The amount of water needed may vary. Therefore, mix the water in slowly and stop once the dough is somewhat sticky, but can be picked up and handled.
KNEAD the dough on a lightly floured surface for 00:05:00 until smooth.
STORE the dough in a plastic bag, making sure the bag is wrapped tightly around the dough. Let sit for 00:25:00 in a dry, warm place. Room temperature 25 %
ROLL dough into a square 0.125 in thick.
CUT into squares approximately 4 in by 4 in .
HEAT 3 cups of vegetable oil to 350 °F - 400 °F For sea level, HEAT 3 cups of vegetable oil to 365 °F - 415 °F
PLACE squares of dough, one at a time, in oil.
FRY until the sopaipilla puffs on one side. Turn and fry until golden brown.
DRAIN on paper towel. Sopaipillas will be hot!
SERVE with honey, powdered sugar, or cinnamon sugar. Or, make stuffed sopaipillas.
creator: Chasz Griego
